title: Cordula villosa
intro: Cordyceps sinensis, also known as Cordyceps, is a type of fungus that grows on the larvae of certain insects, particularly the caterpillars of the Hepialus moth. The health benefits of Cordyceps include improved respiratory function, increased energy levels, and enhanced immune system function, as well as potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of Cordyceps include cordycepin, polysaccharides, and other secondary metabolites, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cordyceps include capsules, tablets, and teas, as well as extracts and powders that can be added to soups and other dishes in traditional Chinese medicine.
